headline: Malaysia's overall governance score dropped 8 points in 3 years — worst in decade
context: Malaysia's composite governance score across Worldwide Governance Indicators, Corruption Perceptions Index, and Open Budget Index dropped 8 points from 2022 to 2025, the worst decline in a decade.

cards.big: Malaysia's governance score dropped 8 points in 3 years — worst decline in a decade
cards.sub: Every major international governance indicator shows the same downward trajectory

cards.big: CPI score fell from 48 to 42 — now below Vietnam and only 3 points above Philippines
cards.sub: Malaysia's anti-corruption reputation declining faster than regional peers

cards.big: Open Budget Index score dropped from 68 to 59 — lowest since methodology began in 2012
cards.sub: Fiscal transparency declining at the same time government spending is increasing

cards.big: World Governance Indicators showed decline in 5 of 6 dimensions for Malaysia
cards.sub: Broad-based deterioration across voice, accountability, effectiveness, rule of law, and corruption

cards.big: An 8-point decline isn't a blip — it's a trend that tells the world Malaysia is moving backwards on governance
Like a student whose grades have dropped every semester for three years — one bad test is a blip, but a trend means something is structurally wrong
cards.sub: An 8-point decline across multiple indices is not noise — it signals systemic governance erosion

cards.big: Can Malaysia reverse its governance decline without structural reform of institutions?
